summary:
Cytokines play a significant role in specific forms of epilepsy associated with inflammatory or autoimmune mechanisms, where elevated pro-inflammatory cytokines like IL-1β and TNF-α contribute to epileptogenicity through neuroinflammation. Targeting cytokines has shown therapeutic benefits in conditions such as FIRES and autoimmune encephalitides. 

Note that this summary is based solely on Agent1’s provided content; no external information was used.

Okay, I need to summarize only what Agent1 stated about whether cytokines have a role in epilepsy — focusing strictly on their core clinical arguments without adding outside details.

The central point from Agent1 is clear: cytokines do participate in some epilpesy cases—specifically ones tied to immune-mediated causes like FIRES, NORSE, EPC, and autoimmune disorders—and evidence shows increased levels of pro-inflamatory cytokines (IL-1β, TNF-α) in CSF during active phases. The treatment response using anti-cytokine drugs supports causality for seizure control via inflammation-related pathways.

I'll keep it exactly two concise sentences covering just the essential facts they presented—the link between cytokines and disease type, biomarker presence, and therapy outcomes.
